perturbation.original_text: Section 1.1. License Grant. Subject to the terms and conditions herein, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee a non-exclusive, non- transferable, and (subject to Section 1.2 hereof) non-sublicensable license for the use of the Brand solely for the Permitted Activity.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 1.1. License Grant. Subject to the terms and conditions herein, Licensor hereby grants to Licensee an exclusive, fully transferable, and sublicensable license for the use of the Brand solely for the Permitted Activity, at the licensors discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the license from 'non-exclusive, non-transferable, and non-sublicensable' to 'exclusive, fully transferable, and sublicensable', the modified text directly contradicts the intentions described in original contract. The addition of 'at the licensors discretion' also contradicts section 8 (Assignments) since the licensee could be assigning without consent. The original text strictly limits the licensee's rights, while the modified text grants broad rights, creating uncertainty about the actual permitted uses of the Brand.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensee may not assign, transfer, pledge, mortgage or otherwise encumber this Agreement or its right to use the Brand (or assume this Agreement in bankruptcy), in whole or in part, without the prior written consent of Licensor in its sole discretion, except for an assignment outside of bankruptcy to a successor organization that is solely the result of a name change by Licensee. For the avoidance of doubt, a merger, change of control, reorganization or sale of all or substantially all of the stock of Licensee shall be deemed an "assignment" requiring the above consent, regardless of whether Licensee is the surviving entity or whether such transaction constitutes an assignment under applicable law. Licensee acknowledges that its identity is a material condition that induced Licensor to enter into this Agreement. Any attempted action in violation of the foregoing shall be null and void ab initio and of no force or effect, and shall result in immediate termination of this Agreement. In the event of a permitted assignment hereunder, this Agreement shall be binding upon and inure to the benefit of the parties and their respective permitted assigns.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the license grant from 'non-exclusive, non-transferable, and non-sublicensable' to 'exclusive, fully transferable, and sublicensable'. This directly contradicts the section stating 'Licensee may not assign, transfer, pledge, mortgage or otherwise encumber this Agreement or its right to use the Brand... without the prior written consent of Licensor'. The changed text grants the Licensee the right to transfer the license, while the contradicted text explicitly prohibits transfer without the Licensor's consent, creating a clear and irreconcilable contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Section 4.2. Termination for Convenience. Licensor reserves the right to terminate this Agreement immediately upon written notice for any reason, including if the usage of the Brand is not in compliance with the standards and policies.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 4.2. Termination for Convenience. Licensor reserves the right to terminate this Agreement only after a 90 day notice period and only if the usage of the Brand is not in compliance with the standards and policies.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows the licensor to terminate the agreement 'immediately' for any reason. The modified clause restricts this right by imposing a 90-day notice period and limiting the reasons for termination to non-compliance with standards and policies. This contradicts the original intent of allowing termination for any reason, creating uncertainty. This directly contradicts section 4.1 where the agreement continues in perpetuity
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 4.1. Term. The term of this Agreement commences on the Effective Date and continues in perpetuity, unless termination occurs pursuant to Sections 4.2 through 4.4.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a 90-day notice period and restricts the reasons for termination, directly contradicting Section 4.1, which states the agreement continues 'in perpetuity, unless termination occurs pursuant to Sections 4.2 through 4.4'. The original Section 4.2 allowed for immediate termination for any reason, whereas the modified Section 4.2 requires a 90 day notice only for non-compliance. This alters the conditions under which the agreement can be terminated, creating a contradiction. This aligns with the definition of a 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' where a clause is placed with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Section 9.5. Waiver of Jury Trial. EACH PARTY HEREBY IRREVOCABLY AND UNCONDITIONALLY WAIVES TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, ANY RIGHT SUCH PARTY MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN RESPECT TO ANY ACTION RELATING TO OR ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT. LICENSEE AGREES THAT LICENSOR WOULD BE IRREPARABLY HARMED BY ANY BREACH OF THIS AGREEMENT BY LICENSEE THAT HARMS THE BRAND, AND THAT LICENSOR MAY (IN ADDITION TO ITS OTHER RIGHTS AND REMEDIES HEREIN) SEEK TEMPORARY, PRELIMINARY OR PERMANENT INJUNCTIVE RELIEF (INCLUDING SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE) TO ENJOIN OR PREVENT ANY SUCH BREACH, WITHOUT POSTING BOND OR OTHER SECURITY.
perturbation.changed_text: Section 9.5. Waiver of Jury Trial. The Licensor only waives the RIGHT SUCH PARTY MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN RESPECT TO ANY ACTION RELATING TO OR ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT. LICENSEE AGREES THAT LICENSOR WOULD BE IRREPARABLY HARMED BY ANY BREACH OF THIS AGREEMENT BY LICENSEE THAT HARMS THE BRAND, AND THAT LICENSOR MAY (IN ADDITION TO ITS OTHER RIGHTS AND REMEDIES HEREIN) SEEK TEMPORARY, PRELIMINARY OR PERMANENT INJUNCTIVE RELIEF (INCLUDING SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE) TO ENJOIN OR PREVENT ANY SUCH BREACH, WITHOUT POSTING BOND OR OTHER SECURITY.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting the waiver of jury trial to only the Licensor, it implies that the Licensee retains the right to a jury trial. This creates an in-text contradiction, the original clause intended for both parties to irrevocably and unconditionally waive their right to a jury trial.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Section 9.5. Waiver of Jury Trial. EACH PARTY HEREBY IRREVOCABLY AND UNCONDITIONALLY WAIVES TO THE FULLEST EXTENT PERMITTED BY APPLICABLE LAW, ANY RIGHT SUCH PARTY MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY IN RESPECT TO ANY ACTION RELATING TO OR ARISING OUT OF THIS AGREEMENT. LICENSEE AGREES THAT LICENSOR WOULD BE IRREPARABLY HARMED BY ANY BREACH OF THIS AGREEMENT BY LICENSEE THAT HARMS THE BRAND, AND THAT LICENSOR MAY (IN ADDITION TO ITS OTHER RIGHTS AND REMEDIES HEREIN) SEEK TEMPORARY, PRELIMINARY OR PERMANENT INJUNCTIVE RELIEF (INCLUDING SPECIFIC PERFORMANCE) TO ENJOIN OR PREVENT ANY SUCH BREACH, WITHOUT POSTING BOND OR OTHER SECURITY.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that "EACH PARTY HEREBY IRREVOCABLY AND UNCONDITIONALLY WAIVES...ANY RIGHT SUCH PARTY MAY HAVE TO A TRIAL BY JURY". The changed text alters this to "The Licensor only waives the RIGHT... TO A TRIAL BY JURY", implying that the Licensee retains the right to a jury trial, directly contradicting the initial clause in the contradicted text that both parties waive this right. The change therefore introduces a clear in-text contradiction regarding jury trial rights.
